title: Blueberry Auto
subtitle: Vehicle Auction & Marketplace Platform
summary: Vehicle auction and marketplace platform for the Bangladesh market. Leading the end-to-end build as the contracted developer.
headline: pnpm monorepo · Vite/React frontend · Hono API · Supabase backend · separate crawler app.
description: Blueberry Auto is a vehicle auction and marketplace platform built from scratch as a pnpm monorepo. I lead architecture, OAuth, Supabase migrations, edge functions, and Vercel deployment.

sections.body: pnpm monorepo: apps/web (Vite + React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui), apps/api (Hono backend), apps/crawler (data ingestion), packages/shared + packages/config, supabase/ migrations.
Contact-Email edge function, Supabase workflow, Google OAuth security refactor, exchange-rate calculator, notifications system.

title: Opti Retreat Voting System
subtitle: Internal Voting App for Optimizely Retreat
summary: Internal voting application built for Optimizely's annual company retreat; React + TypeScript SPA used by the Bangladesh office.
headline: Shipped internal tool · React + TypeScript SPA on Netlify.
description: A React + TypeScript single-page voting app used internally at Optimizely's 2024 company retreat in the Bangladesh office.

title: GCMAF
subtitle: Medical Product & Research Site
summary: Marketing and research site for a medical product line; led design and build.
headline: Next.js 16 on Cloudflare Workers · Resend for transactional email · Turnstile for form protection.
description: GCMAF is a marketing and research site for a medical product line, deployed on Cloudflare Workers with Next.js. I led the design and build.

sections.body: Clinical applications, product, research, contact, and about pages.

title: DBH Services
subtitle: Corporate / Holding Company Website
summary: Corporate website for a holding company covering home, services, allied businesses, and admin contact submissions.
headline: Vite + React + shadcn/ui · react-hook-form + zod · recharts · framer-motion.
description: DBH Services is a corporate website for a holding company. I led the design and build.

sections.body: Home, About, Services, Allied Business Concerns, Contact + admin Contact Submissions, Privacy, Terms.

title: NavA11y
subtitle: Dynamic WCAG 2.4 Accessibility Testing
summary: Dynamic WCAG 2.4 accessibility testing tool — the first to cover all five focus-behavior success criteria. Drives a real browser to simulate keyboard navigation and inspect rendered page state at each focus event.
headline: 100% precision and recall on the labeled benchmark · 2,947 violations detected on 26 production sites · 90% confirmed true-positive rate.
description: NavA11y is a dynamic analysis tool for automated WCAG 2.4 accessibility evaluation built around Playwright-driven browser automation. Unlike static checkers (axe-core, Lighthouse) that inspect HTML/ARIA attributes, NavA11y drives a real Chromium browser to actually execute keyboard navigation and capture rendered focus state — surfacing violations that only appear at runtime.

sections.body: Page-Level Analysis performs full-keyboard tab traversal and computes Kendall τ divergence between expected and observed focus order. Element-Level Analysis inspects each focused element's rendered focus state, including a 7×7 grid obscuration probe to detect occluded focus indicators.

100% precision and recall on a 22-page labeled benchmark dataset (32 SC-level assertions). Detected 2,947 violations across 26 production websites; 90% precision confirmed via independent manual validation by two expert raters (PABAK = 0.80).

First automated tool to cover all five WCAG 2.4 focus-behavior success criteria. Released a reusable 22-page labeled focus-behavior dataset covering pass, boundary, and obscuration cases for community benchmarking.

Saifullah, Zerin, Begum, Sakib. "NavA11y: A Dynamic Analysis Approach for WCAG 2.4 Focus-Behavior Evaluation." In Proceedings of ENASE 2026 — International Conference on Evaluation of Novel Approaches to Software Engineering. Accepted.

title: Axiom Migration
subtitle: Platform Migration to @optiaxiom/react
summary: Multi-year migration from a legacy design library to Axiom — adapter components, component migrations, and dark-mode theming.
headline: Adapter components · component migrations · Authored internal guides adopted across product teams.
description: Axiom is Optimizely's open-source design system (`@optiaxiom/react`). My contributions span the full migration lifecycle of the platform.

sections.body: Authored React/TypeScript adapter components that allowed the platform to incrementally migrate from the legacy library while preserving existing call-sites. Contributed component migrations directly in the design-system repo.
Authored internal migration guides used across product teams.
Refactored Dark Mode to CSS variables; centralized fragmented status indicators into a unified status schema; added missing i18n locale variants.

title: AI Platform Integration
subtitle: AI SDK Integration + Block-based Document UI
summary: End-to-end integration of an AI SDK across the platform — service migration, internal publishing pipeline, instrumentation, and a block-based AI document UI.
headline: AI-context events across multiple product surfaces · graduated feature from beta to GA.
description: I worked on the end-to-end AI integration into the platform, including infrastructure migration, the AI event-tracking layer, and a block-based AI document interface.

sections.body: Migrated the AI service to a modern framework. Designed the internal package publishing pipeline. Built the permissions layer and test suite. Authored internal documentation that helped graduate the feature from beta to GA.
Built a reusable event-tracking hook and instrumented structured AI-context events across multiple product surfaces.
Implemented backend context extraction for LLM prompts, frontend block form interaction, and AI resource rendering for the platform's AI document interface.

title: Clippy
subtitle: Smart PDF Reader for Researchers
summary: Smart PDF reader with knowledge graph extraction, citation network visualization, and paper recommendation. Submitted to ICSE 2023 Student Research Competition.
headline: 392 commits · 5 GitHub stars · Top 6 at ICSE SRC 2023.
description: Clippy reimagines the academic PDF reader: papers are parsed into a knowledge graph, citations form an interactive network, and similar papers are recommended via embedding similarity.

sections.body: PDF parsing → knowledge graph extraction; citation network visualization; paper recommendation engine; OTP-based authentication; Azure CI/CD pipeline.
Selected as a Top 6 finalist at the ICSE 2023 Student Research Competition (SCORE) — an international undergraduate SE research competition.

title: Mr. Bin
subtitle: Recycling Marketplace + ML Classification
summary: Full-stack recycling and exchange marketplace with ML-based object detection for item classification.
headline: MERN frontend with a Spring Boot 3 / Java 17 backend rewrite.
description: Mr. Bin is a two-sided marketplace connecting recyclers and sellers of usable products. Built originally on MERN, later partially rewritten to Spring Boot 3 / Java 17.

sections.body: User auth, listings, real-time chat, geolocation-based discovery via Google Maps API, ML-based image classification of recyclable materials.
Initial MERN implementation; backend later rewritten in Spring Boot 3 / Java 17 as part of Therap's JavaFest 2023 entry.

title: API Miners GPT
subtitle: GPT-Powered PDF Chatbot
summary: GPT-powered PDF chatbot with voice input, OCR, and Stable Diffusion image generation.
headline: Won Best UI/UX at BUET Hackathon 2023.
description: A multimodal GPT interface: chat with PDFs, voice input via the Web Speech API, OCR for image-to-text, and on-the-fly image generation via Stable Diffusion.

sections.body: PDF chat (RAG-style), voice input via the Web Speech API, OCR pipeline, AI image generation via Stable Diffusion, integrated bookstore.
.NET backend with SQL Server, React + Tailwind CSS frontend.

title: MiniFacebook
subtitle: Monolith → Microservices
summary: Demonstrated monolith-to-microservices decomposition — split a Node.js app into User, Story, and Status services behind an NGINX reverse proxy.
headline: NGINX-fronted Docker Compose stack with three independent services.
description: An academic study in service decomposition: take a Node.js monolith and break it cleanly along user/story/status seams, with NGINX handling routing and Docker Compose handling orchestration.

sections.body: Three independent services (User, Story, Status) each with their own data store; NGINX reverse proxy for routing and basic load balancing; Docker Compose for orchestration.

title: BPE Tokenizer
subtitle: Byte Pair Encoding from Scratch
summary: Byte Pair Encoding tokenizer implemented from scratch and published as python-bpe-tokenizer on PyPI. Includes a Flask web interface for training on custom corpora.
headline: Direct precursor to my SEMERU Lab tokenizer research.
description: A from-scratch BPE tokenizer that handles the full training/encoding/decoding cycle. Shipped as a public PyPI package and a Flask UI for non-CLI users.

sections.body: Published as python-bpe-tokenizer on PyPI — supports training on custom corpora, tokenize/detokenize workflows, and vocabulary export.
Built as a teaching tool and as a direct precursor to the PL-specific tokenizer research at SEMERU Lab.

title: EmitXplorer
subtitle: Greenhouse Gas Visualization
summary: Gamified greenhouse gas visualization platform using SCISAT satellite data and ML-based CO₂ concentration prediction.
headline: Built for NASA Space Apps Challenge 2023.
description: EmitXplorer turns SCISAT satellite emissions data into an interactive gamified visualization, with an ML model that predicts CO₂ concentrations from input parameters.

sections.body: SCISAT satellite data → preprocessing → ML model (TensorFlow) for CO₂ prediction → React/ASP.NET visualization with gamified exploration.

title: ML Bug Mining (SZZ)
subtitle: Mining Software Repositories
summary: Mined ML-related bugs across 9 open-source repositories using the SZZ algorithm.
headline: 9 repos · bug-fixing → bug-introducing commit linkage.
description: An MSR (Mining Software Repositories) project: applied the SZZ algorithm to identify bug-fixing and bug-introducing commits across 9 ML-library-touching open-source projects.

sections.body: Clone repos → keyword-heuristic bug-fix detection → git blame to recover bug-introducing commits → filter for ML-library-touching code → analysis.
